title: Mainframe DevOps: Gulp, Zowe Endevor & Mocha

steps.title: Initialize the Project
hints: Run `npm init -y` to create package.json with default values.
Install all tools in one command: `npm install --save-dev gulp mocha chai sinon`

steps.title: Create Your First Gulpfile
hints: Create `gulpfile.js` in your workspace root. Gulp 4 tasks are plain exported async functions â€” no separate registration step needed.
The minimum valid gulpfile: `const gulp = require('gulp'); async function hello() { console.log('Hello!'); } exports.default = hello;`

steps.title: Configure Zowe for Endevor
hints: The `zowe.config.json` template has been copied to your workspace. Fill in the `host` (and optionally `port`) for your Endevor REST API server.

The `endevor-location` profile specifies the inventory location â€” set `instance`, `environment`, `system`, and `subsystem` to match your site. Placeholder values are fine if you don't have access yet.

steps.title: Build the EndevorService Class
hints: Each method should call `execAsync()` with the matching `zowe endevor` command and use `this.buildBaseArgs()` to append the common flags.
For `listElements()`: the `--rfj` flag makes Zowe return JSON â€” parse `stdout` with `JSON.parse()` and return `result.data`. The other two methods don't need to parse the output.

steps.title: Wire Endevor Into Gulp
hints: Import your service at the top of gulpfile.js: `const { EndevorService } = require('./src/endevor');` then create one service instance from environment variables.
Write three async Gulp task functions â€” `listElements`, `retrieveElement`, and `addElement` â€” that each call the corresponding service method. Export each one.

steps.title: Test EndevorService with Mocha & Sinon
hints: Stub `child_process.exec` so tests never actually call Zowe CLI: `sinon.stub(childProcess, 'exec').yields(null, fakeStdout, '');` â€” call `sinon.restore()` in `afterEach`.
For `listElements()`, the fake stdout must be valid JSON matching Zowe's response shape: `JSON.stringify({ data: [{ elmName: 'HELLOPGM' }] })`

steps.title: Add Error Handling and Error-Path Tests
hints: Wrap each `execAsync` call in a try/catch that re-throws an `Error` with a human-readable message: `throw new Error('Failed to list elements: ' + (err.stderr || err.message))`
In your test, make the stub yield an error: `sinon.stub(childProcess, 'exec').yields(new Error('Connection refused'), '', 'Connection refused');` then assert the thrown message contains your prefix.

steps.title: Build the Deployment Pipeline
hints: Use `gulp.series(clean, listElements, retrieveElement, runTests, addElement)` to define the pipeline â€” tasks run sequentially, each waiting for the previous to finish.
Export the pipeline as `deploy` and set it as the default: `const deploy = gulp.series(...); exports.deploy = deploy; exports.default = deploy;`
